explanation:
Unit conversions are the foundation of science, engineering, and everyday life. The metric system (SI) is the global standard. The United States and some territories maintain the imperial system.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the standard torque conversion formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values that seem unusually high or low.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, or legal matters), consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What is torque and why does it matter?
faq.a: Torque is the rotational force applied to an object. It's critical in automotive, machinery, and mechanical engineering because it determines how much rotational power a motor or engine can deliver.

faq.q: What's the difference between torque and horsepower?
faq.a: Torque is the rotational force; horsepower is the rate at which that force is applied (torque × RPM). Both matter for vehicle performance.

faq.q: What torque units are most common?
faq.a: Newton-meters (Nm) is the SI standard used worldwide. Foot-pounds (lb·ft) is common in the US. Kilogram-meters (kg·m) is occasionally used in older equipment.

faq.q: How much torque does a typical car wheel need?
faq.a: Most car wheel lug nuts are torqued between 90–140 Nm (66–103 lb·ft), depending on the vehicle.

faq.q: Why do diesel engines have more torque?
faq.a: Diesel engines produce maximum torque at lower RPMs than gasoline engines, making them ideal for towing and heavy loads.

faq.q: What is a torque wrench and how do I use it?
faq.a: A torque wrench is a tool that ensures fasteners are tightened to a specific torque value, preventing over-tightening or under-tightening. Set the desired torque, apply steady pressure until it clicks or beeps.

faq.q: Why do electric vehicles have instant torque?
faq.a: Electric motors deliver maximum torque from 0 RPM, unlike internal combustion engines that build torque gradually as RPMs increase.
